text: Last class I wrote a clear, polite email and signed off with Yours sincerely.
Now I have a reply to write, and I want it to be short and polite.
Acknowledge
Apologize
Clarification
Certainly
Unfortunately
Query
Promptly
Sender
Have you sent the report? Yes, I have.
Can you send it again? Certainly, I can.
Did you read my query? Yes, I did.
Can you meet on Monday? Unfortunately, I cannot.
Percival, I have written the reply to the sender, Mr. Costa. Have you read it?
Yes, I have. It is clear. Did you acknowledge his message at the start?
Yes, I did. I thanked him for answering so promptly. To his query, I said certainly, I can send a PDF.
Good. And the Monday meeting? Can you make it?
Unfortunately, I cannot in the morning, so I offered the afternoon. I also apologized for the file problem.
Perfect. It answers every question and offers clarification. You can send it now.
Thank you for answering so promptly.
Certainly, I can send the file again.
Unfortunately, I cannot meet on Monday morning.
I do apologize for the delay.
Please let me know if you need any clarification.

Hi Percival, this is Karen. I am reading you the reply I have just written to Mr. Costa, so you can check it before I send it. The subject line is: Re: Supplier report -- for your review. Dear Mr. Costa, thank you for your email and for your prompt answer. Yes, of course, I can send the report in a different format. I have attached it again as a PDF, so the file should open now. To answer your query about the audit, certainly, the audit team has the same version. Unfortunately, I cannot meet on Monday morning, but I am free in the afternoon. I do apologize for the small problem with the first file. Please let me know if you need any clarification. Kind regards, Karen. That is the whole reply. Please tell me if it sounds polite enough before I send it to the sender.

Hello, this is Daniel from the audit team. I am leaving a short follow-up about Karen's reply. Thank you, we have received the new file, and yes, it opens fine now. To acknowledge your message: certainly, Monday afternoon works for us. There is one small query from my side. Could Karen send the summary page on its own as well? Unfortunately, one of our colleagues cannot open the full PDF on her old computer. There is no rush, so please reply when you have a moment. And there is no need to apologize for the first file. These things happen. Thank you again, and have a good day.

First, Karen thanks the sender for the email and for the prompt answer. Then, she answers the first question and says yes, of course, she can send a new file. After that, she replies to the query about the audit and confirms certainly. Later, she says unfortunately she cannot meet on Monday morning, but offers the afternoon. Finally, she apologizes briefly for the problem and offers help with any clarification.
Acknowledge
Apologize
Clarification
Certainly
Unfortunately
Query
Promptly
Sender
Certainly, I can send the file again.
Unfortunately, I cannot meet on Monday morning.
To answer your query, the file is a PDF.
I do apologize for the delay.
Have you read my reply? Yes, I have.
Please let me know if you need any clarification.
Thank you for answering so promptly.
